creator: sweeney@addr.com
text: It's hard to argue with that.  My personal take as a release engineer is that even though the code is final, blatant documentation errata could and should be fixed in line, since of necessity they become final after the remainder of the software.  In this case it doesn't matter, since the text is merely descriptive. But if the text were intended to be cut and pasted then it would merit a post hoc fix, in my view.
